message.content:
In /home/nick/work/libnn, run these commands and return ALL output verbatim:

1. `.venv/bin/python3 scripts/optdb.py search "maxpool" --limit 10`
2. `.venv/bin/python3 scripts/optdb.py search "avepool" --limit 10`
3. `.venv/bin/python3 scripts/optdb.py search "lmul memory bound" --limit 10`
4. `git show 3cc063eb --stat` (avepool e16 commit)
5. `git log --oneline | grep -i "maxpool\|pool" | head -10`

content.text: I'll run these commands for you.

content.text: Let me check the optdb.py usage first and retry without the invalid flag:

input.description: Search optdb for lmul memory bound
Show git log for pool-related commits

content.content:
--- [4f167833] (score: 0.352) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: regressed
  Speedup: 0.97x (3.2% regression)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: Unrolled k_x by 2 to interleave vle8/vmax — REGRESSED
  Approach: Unrolled inner k_x loop by 2, loading into V8 and V16, then vmaxing both against V0. Goal was to interleave loads and computation to hide load-to-use latency.

--- [77473db5] (score: 0.324) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 1.012x (1.2% improvement, essentially neutral)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: Replaced inner loop multiply-based addressing with precomputed pointer + stride addition
  Approach: Precomputed row_ptr base address before k_y loop and used col_ptr += in_tensor_ch for k_x advancement, eliminating the multiply (k_x + k_y * in_tensor_dim) * in_tensor_ch in the inner loop.

--- [fb9cda57] (score: 0.297) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 0.99x (neutral/slight regression)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: M4 2-pixel parallel — neutral, memory-bound at M8 already
  Approach: Dropped LMUL from M8 to M4, processed 2 output pixels in parallel (separate accumulators v0/v4, separate load registers v8/v12). Goal: double memory-level parallelism. Result: IPC improved 0.32→0.57 but instruction count doubled (8192→14584). Net cycles: 25805 vs 25667 baseline (slight regression). The A/B pixel kernel loops are sequential so no true load interleaving occurs.

--- [78371ff0] (score: 0.247) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Date: 2026-03-24
  Outcome: improved
  Speedup: 1.57x
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Summary: Split im2col into top/mid/bottom regions with bulk row copies for pad=0 center — 1.57x speedup
  Approach: Replaced single im2col loop with 5-region split (top/mid-left/mid-center/mid-right/bottom). Mid-center uses nn_dup_s8(in_tensor_ch * ker_dim_x) bulk row copy instead of per-position nn_dup_s8(in_tensor_ch). For pad=0 test case (32x30x64, 3x3, out_ch=16), all pixels fall in mid-center. Reduces 9 copies of 64B to 3 copies of 192B per pixel. Also fixed boundary clamping bug where bound_x_end could exceed out_tensor_dim_x.

--- [d7f18ece] (score: 0.219) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Date: 2026-03-24
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 1.004x (neutral)
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Summary: 8-pixel double-pump batching (im2col 8 pixels, call unroll4 twice) — neutral (0.4%)
  Approach: Changed im2col buffer from 4 to 8 columns. When 8 columns ready, call unroll4 kernel twice for pixels 0-3 then 4-7. Hypothesis: weight data (9KB per call) stays in L1 for second call. Reality: weights were already in L1 with 4-pixel batching. Added drain_ptr logic for 1..7 pixel leftover handling.

--- [4f167833] (score: 0.243) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: regressed
  Speedup: 0.97x (3.2% regression)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: Unrolled k_x by 2 to interleave vle8/vmax — REGRESSED
  Approach: Unrolled inner k_x loop by 2, loading into V8 and V16, then vmaxing both against V0. Goal was to interleave loads and computation to hide load-to-use latency.

--- [fb9cda57] (score: 0.183) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 0.99x (neutral/slight regression)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: M4 2-pixel parallel — neutral, memory-bound at M8 already
  Approach: Dropped LMUL from M8 to M4, processed 2 output pixels in parallel (separate accumulators v0/v4, separate load registers v8/v12). Goal: double memory-level parallelism. Result: IPC improved 0.32→0.57 but instruction count doubled (8192→14584). Net cycles: 25805 vs 25667 baseline (slight regression). The A/B pixel kernel loops are sequential so no true load interleaving occurs.

--- [77473db5] (score: 0.183) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 1.012x (1.2% improvement, essentially neutral)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: Replaced inner loop multiply-based addressing with precomputed pointer + stride addition
  Approach: Precomputed row_ptr base address before k_y loop and used col_ptr += in_tensor_ch for k_x advancement, eliminating the multiply (k_x + k_y * in_tensor_dim) * in_tensor_ch in the inner loop.

--- [d27c0808] (score: 0.179) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: improved
  Speedup: 4.63x
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast
  Summary: Replaced ENA_TILING GEMM with im2col + 4x4 vd4dots GEMM — 4.63x speedup
  Approach: Same pattern. nn_mat_mul_kernel_q7_q15_bias_2sft_unroll4 (signed*signed vd4dots).

--- [6ef161b2] (score: 0.158) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: improved
  Speedup: 3.91x
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast
  Summary: Replaced ENA_TILING GEMM with im2col + 4x4 vd4dotsu GEMM for u8 variant — 3.91x speedup
  Approach: Same mechanical pattern as s8 variants: removed weight transpose + tiling GEMM (nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_bias_u8_u8_s8, vectorizes col2=32, 12.5% VL util at VLEN=1024), replaced with 4-pixel im2col + nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft_unroll4 (4x4 vd4dotsu, vectorizes K=1152). u8-specific: vd4dotsu for signed*unsigned dot product, vnclipu for [0,255] clipping, NDS_ISA_SAT for scalar fallback.

--- [fb9cda57] (score: 0.430) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 0.99x (neutral/slight regression)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: M4 2-pixel parallel — neutral, memory-bound at M8 already
  Approach: Dropped LMUL from M8 to M4, processed 2 output pixels in parallel (separate accumulators v0/v4, separate load registers v8/v12). Goal: double memory-level parallelism. Result: IPC improved 0.32→0.57 but instruction count doubled (8192→14584). Net cycles: 25805 vs 25667 baseline (slight regression). The A/B pixel kernel loops are sequential so no true load interleaving occurs.

--- [conv-hwc-v4] (score: 0.400) ---
  Function: conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: improved
  Speedup: 3.09x (vs 2.47x for v2)
  Function: conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Summary: 4x2 tiled GEMM kernel (v4) replaces 2x2/M4 for small-channel convolutions
  Approach: 4-channel x 2-pixel tiling with LMUL=M2 instead of 2x2/M4. Processes 4 output channels in inner loop, 2 pixels in outer unroll.

--- [d7f18ece] (score: 0.356) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Date: 2026-03-24
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 1.004x (neutral)
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Summary: 8-pixel double-pump batching (im2col 8 pixels, call unroll4 twice) — neutral (0.4%)
  Approach: Changed im2col buffer from 4 to 8 columns. When 8 columns ready, call unroll4 kernel twice for pixels 0-3 then 4-7. Hypothesis: weight data (9KB per call) stays in L1 for second call. Reality: weights were already in L1 with 4-pixel batching. Added drain_ptr logic for 1..7 pixel leftover handling.

--- [31ed3665] (score: 0.318) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Date: 2026-03-23
  Outcome: improved
  Speedup: 1.03x
  Function: riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
  Summary: Upgraded GEMM from 4x2/M2 (v4) to 4x4/M1 (unroll4) for out_ch<=64 path — 2.6% improvement
  Approach: Added nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_unroll4 with 4-channel x 4-pixel tiling using LMUL=M1. 16 M1 accumulators, 8 loads + 16 vd4dots per inner loop iteration. Compute-to-load ratio 2.0 (vs 1.33 for v4). Modified conv function to buffer 4 im2col pixels instead of 2.

--- [77473db5] (score: 0.310) ---
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Date: 2026-03-21
  Outcome: neutral
  Speedup: 1.012x (1.2% improvement, essentially neutral)
  Function: riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8
  Summary: Replaced inner loop multiply-based addressing with precomputed pointer + stride addition
  Approach: Precomputed row_ptr base address before k_y loop and used col_ptr += in_tensor_ch for k_x advancement, eliminating the multiply (k_x + k_y * in_tensor_dim) * in_tensor_ch in the inner loop.

commit 3cc063eb43a809fbd9d3e170a287bb8b58f37365
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 23 08:45:33 2026 +0800

    [opt/rvv] Add e16 accumulation path for avepool_HWC_s8 — 2.79x speedup
    
    For small kernels (count <= 256), use e16/M4 with vwadd instead of e32/M2
    with vqmacc, doubling VL from 256 to 512 at VLEN=1024. The e16 range is
    sufficient since count*127 fits in s16 for typical pooling windows.
    
    FPGA results: 149,131 → 53,535 cycles (2.79x speedup)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Source/PoolingFunctions/riscv_nn_avepool_HWC_s8.c | 76 ++++++++++++++++-------
 1 file changed, 55 insertions(+), 21 deletions(-)

3cc063eb [opt/rvv] Add e16 accumulation path for avepool_HWC_s8 — 2.79x speedup
8bc11106 [unit test] Adjust some paramters in the unit tests of avepool_HWC_s8_asym and avepool_HWC_s16_asym to make the target with VLEN=2048 reaching 100% code coverage.
340e1d10 [unit test] Add the lost semicolon into the unit test of some pooling functions.
cdcd7969 [um] Update the doxygen version user manual for pooling group.
20a54b0e [misc] Rename some variables in avepool_HWC_[s8|s16]_asym.
38e989fc [unit test] Adjust some parameters in the unit test of avepool_HWC_s8_asym to v-ext algorithm reaching 100% code coverage.
ce54e38d [misc] Update the RVV algorithm in avepool_HWC_s16_asym to follow the latest reference code.
5b41b1e1 [misc] Update the RV64P algorithm in avepool_HWC_s16_asym to follow the latest reference code.
19b5fac2 [misc] Update the RV32P algorithm in avepool_HWC_s16_asym to follow the latest reference code.
19a9dde9 [misc] Update the plain C algorithm in avepool_HWC_s16_asym to follow the latest reference code.
